behavior_summary: Implements the enchant action applied to a passed item: validates the item's Q field, maps it into an enchant category (Q-0x50), and returns early for reserved Q values (0x51..0x56).
Scans the acting NPC's equipment and nearby/related items to classify candidate reagents/targets and builds a bitmask describing which reagent/target classes are present.

Consumes reagent stacks (decrementing or destroying Item instances), creates new item instances (Item::create) and places them into the world (Item::pop) at positions computed from the enchanted item's X/Y/Z, incrementing an internal offset per created item.

Enforces mana cost on the actor (checks Npc::getMana and applies Npc::setMana), plays sound effects, spawns multiple short-lived visual/timing helper processes (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::091C, FREE::*), and calls AccumulateIntelligence on success.
Cleans up temporaries and spawned helper objects (Item::destroy and FREE helpers) and returns when preconditions (valid equip/reagent or supported Q category) are not met.
notable_control_flow: Early-guard: if Item::getQ returns a value in 0x51..0x56 the routine bypasses enchant processing and returns immediately (guard path).
Category dispatch: Q-0x50 is used as a small index (1..5 inferred) to select parameter mappings (BP-0A/BP-0C) and which reagent/target classes to look for.

Scan-and-classify loop: iterates NPC equipment and an item list, calling Item::getType, Item::getFrame and a predicate (PENT::0F4B) on each candidate to set bits in a BP-19 bitmask that govern which enchant outcome(s) are allowed.

Consume-or-create sequence: when a matching reagent/target is found, code decrements Item::getQuantity or destroys the item, then creates new items and pops them into the world while spawning visual/sound effects and applying mana/stat changes.
Multiple helper spawns and delayed/free helpers are used to sequence visual effects and cleanup (METHOD::15E7 / METHOD::091C / FREE::1A96 / FREE::29D8 / FREE::1C19).
open_questions: Exact semantics of Item Q values: what in-game item actions correspond to each Q-0x50 category index (numeric mapping inferred but not identified to gameplay labels).
Precise behavior of PENT::0F4B: the predicate's classification rules are inferred from usage (mapping to specific bit flags) but its exact checks are unknown.
Meaning of the numeric BP-0A and BP-0C parameters derived from the switch: are they spawn counts, frame modifiers, or Z-offsets for created items?
Which equip slots or inventory subsets Npc::getEquip is scanning (slot range inferred but not explicit) and whether multi-slot scanning affects the bitmask build order.
Fine-grained timing and visual composition of spawned helpers (METHOD::15E7, METHOD::091C, FREE::*): are they particle effects, UI flashes, or other animation types?

main_function.name: U8P_ENCHANT::ENCHANT
main_function.purpose: Handle a player's/NPC's enchant action on an item: validate and classify the requested enchant, locate and consume necessary reagents/targets, spawn created items and visual/sound effects, deduct mana, and award intelligence/stat progression on success.
entry_conditions: Called when an enchant action is attempted on an item (item pointer passed in BP+06h).
Requires the item's Q field to be outside the reserved bypass range (0x51..0x56).
Requires at least one valid equipped item or candidate reagent/target to be present; otherwise the function returns early.

function_map.name: U8P_ENCHANT::ENCHANT
function_map.role: Main handler for the enchant action on an item (process entry that performs validation, scanning, consumption/creation, effects, and stat/mana updates).

function_map.name: METHOD::15E7
function_map.role: Spawned visual-effect spawner used repeatedly during successful enchant sequences (particle/animation helper).

function_map.name: METHOD::091C
function_map.role: Spawned short-lived helper invoked early in the enchant sequence (likely a brief visual or UI helper).

function_map.name: FREE::1A96
function_map.role: Delay/timing helper called before AccumulateIntelligence to sequence stat accumulation or delayed effects.

function_map.name: FREE::29D8
function_map.role: Spawned helper for an additional global visual/sound effect after parts of the enchant flow.

function_map.name: FREE::1C19
function_map.role: Delayed cleanup/freer helper used to free temporaries or manage timed object cleanup.

function_map.name: PENT::0F4B
function_map.role: Predicate/function used to classify candidate items during scanning; its return maps candidates to bitmask flags used by the enchant decision logic.

function_map.name: Item::*
function_map.role: Collection of item-instance methods used heavily (getQ/getX/getY/getZ/getType/getFrame/getQuantity/setQuantity/create/pop/destroy/bark etc.) to inspect, modify, spawn and move item objects.

function_map.name: Npc::*
function_map.role: NPC methods used to read equip and mana state (Npc::getEquip, Npc::getMana, Npc::setMana, Npc::getDir) used for reagent lookup and effect/mana enforcement.

function_map.name: AccumulateIntelligence
function_map.role: Applies or records intelligence/stat increases after a successful enchant operation.

function_map.name: U8P_ENCHANT::playSFX
function_map.role: Plays sound effects associated with enchant initiation or outcomes.

external_calls.purpose: Read the enchanted item's Q/quality field and map Q-0x50 into the enchant category; used as an early guard and category index.
Obtain the item's X coordinate to compute spawn/pop positions for created items.
Obtain the item's Y coordinate to compute spawn/pop positions for created items (BP-0F stores item Y plus offset).
Obtain the item's Z coordinate (stored in BP-0D) for world spawning/pop placement.
Retrieve equipped items to search for reagents/targets; absence triggers early return.
Read candidate item type during scan to classify reagent/target eligibility.
Read candidate item frame during scan to classify reagent/target eligibility.
Call a predicate on candidate items; its boolean/return value is used to map candidates to specific bitmask flags for enchant decisions.
Read reagent stack counts to determine whether to decrement or destroy the reagent item.
Decrement reagent stacks after consumption by the enchant.
Instantiate new items (enchanted outcome or temporary pop targets) with supplied type/frame derived from category mapping.
Place created items into the world at computed X/Y/Z coordinates; used for spawned results/visuals.
Destroy temporary items or reagent items when their quantity reaches zero after consumption.
Play sound effects to accompany enchant start, reagent consumption, or outcome events.
Check the actor's mana pool to determine if the enchant can proceed under mana cost constraints.
Deduct mana cost from the actor after a successful enchant operation.
Read actor direction and pass it to spawned visual/effect helpers for directional visuals.
Apply or record intelligence/stat gain after successful enchant sequences.
Spawned repeatedly to produce per-frame visual effects at the actor's position/direction during the enchant outcome.
Spawned early in the sequence as a brief visual/UI helper invoked during enchant initiation.
Timing/delay helper invoked prior to calling AccumulateIntelligence to sequence stat gain.
Spawned helper for additional global visual/sound effect after some operations in the enchant flow.
Spawned cleanup/timer helper used to free temporaries or handle delayed cleanup of created objects/effects.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Implements the enchant action applied to a passed item: validates the item's Q field, maps it into an enchant category (Q-0x50), and returns early for reserved Q values (0x51..0x56).
